title: Brand-New Warehouse VLAN Gets No DHCP Addresses at All

ticket.summary: IT stood up a new warehouse subnet (VLAN 220, 10.90.20.0/24) yesterday for handheld barcode scanners. None of the scanners can get an IP address -- they all sit at 'Acquiring network address' indefinitely. The scanners work fine when tested on other, existing VLANs. DHCP is centralized at the datacenter and this is a routed (L3) remote site.

topology.description: The warehouse site connects to the datacenter over a WAN link. Handheld scanners live on VLAN 220 (10.90.20.0/24), terminated on the warehouse L3 switch sw-wh-core. All DHCP for the company is centralized at dhcp-01 (10.1.1.5) in the datacenter -- there is no local DHCP server at the warehouse, so every VLAN's SVI must relay broadcast DHCP requests to dhcp-01 across the WAN. Other warehouse VLANs (e.g. VLAN 210, wired workstations) were configured months ago and work correctly.

logs.content:
Jul 13 09:10:02 sw-wh-core: %DHCP-6-BROADCAST_DROPPED: Broadcast DHCPDISCOVER on Vlan220 dropped, no ip helper-address configured on interface Vlan220
Jul 13 09:10:19 sw-wh-core: %DHCP-6-BROADCAST_DROPPED: Broadcast DHCPDISCOVER on Vlan220 dropped, no ip helper-address configured on interface Vlan220
Jul 13 09:11:47 sw-wh-core: %DHCP-6-BROADCAST_DROPPED: Broadcast DHCPDISCOVER on Vlan220 dropped, no ip helper-address configured on interface Vlan220

09:00:00 -- 10:00:00: No DHCPDISCOVER packets received from relay agent 10.90.20.1 (VLAN 220 SVI) in the last hour
09:00:00 -- 10:00:00: DHCPDISCOVER packets from relay agent 10.90.10.1 (VLAN 210 SVI): 34, all offers accepted

cli_outputs.output:
interface Vlan220
 description Warehouse-Scanners
 ip address 10.90.20.1 255.255.255.0
 no ip helper-address
 ip pim sparse-mode

interface Vlan210
 description Warehouse-Workstations
 ip address 10.90.10.1 255.255.255.0
 ip helper-address 10.1.1.5
 ip pim sparse-mode

Interface: wlan0
State: DISCOVER (retry 9)
Last offer received: never
Lease: none

monitoring.notes: The relay-agent dashboard on dhcp-01 shows steady DHCPDISCOVER volume from every existing warehouse VLAN's relay agent except one: VLAN 220's SVI (10.90.20.1) has sent exactly zero relayed packets since the VLAN was created yesterday at 08:45, even though the switch's local interface counters confirm scanners are broadcasting DHCPDISCOVER on that VLAN continuously.

hints: Compare the SVI configuration for the new VLAN 220 against a working warehouse VLAN like VLAN 210 -- what's different?
The switch syslog is explicitly telling you why DHCPDISCOVER broadcasts aren't going anywhere on Vlan220.
The Vlan220 SVI on sw-wh-core is missing 'ip helper-address 10.1.1.5' entirely, so DHCP broadcasts never leave the local VLAN and can never reach the centralized dhcp-01 across the WAN.

solution.root_cause: The Vlan220 SVI on sw-wh-core was created without an ip helper-address / DHCP relay pointing at the centralized DHCP server, unlike the working Vlan210 SVI which has ip helper-address 10.1.1.5 configured. DHCP is a broadcast protocol that doesn't route across L3 boundaries on its own, so scanner DHCPDISCOVER broadcasts are dropped locally and never reach dhcp-01.
solution.fix: Add 'ip helper-address 10.1.1.5' to the Vlan220 interface configuration on sw-wh-core so DHCP broadcasts are relayed as unicast to the datacenter DHCP server, matching the working VLAN 210 configuration.
